tickets.title: Tickets
tickets.subtitle: Operational queue with workflow actions
tickets.empty: No tickets match the current filters

links.title: Links
links.search_placeholder: Search by code, title, or body…

comments.attach_files: Drag files here or click to attach
comments.attach_hint: Up to 25 MB per file. Files inherit the comment's visibility — private comments → private files.

review.title: Review Tickets
review.subtitle: Pending tickets that still need triage, metadata review, and sector routing
review.empty_pending: No tickets are waiting for review.
review.empty_reviewed: No tickets have been routed yet.
review.sector_required: Pick a sector to route this ticket
review.sector_tooltip: Distributors hand the ticket to a sector. The sector chief picks the operator unless you're an admin or chief yourself.
review.assignee_hint: You can route to a sector; the chief assigns the operator.
review.private_only_staff: Private — only staff working on the ticket will see this.
review.close_confirm_description: Use this when the request isn't actionable. The routing reason becomes the cancellation justification.
review.moved_past_review: This ticket has already moved past the review queue.
review.triage_actions_not_applicable: Triage actions may not be applicable.

charts.created_vs_closed_hint: Daily ticket creation and closure counts visible to your role.

system.no_active: No active or recently-failed tasks. Things look quiet.

tickets.search: Search by ticket code, title, or description text. The search works together with filters and sorting, so you can narrow a queue without losing your current role-based visibility rules.
tickets.filters: Use status, priority, and sector filters to focus the list. Reset clears the current filter set; the backend still enforces visibility, so hidden sectors or private tickets never appear.
tickets.create: Create a new ticket from here. Internal users create internal beneficiary requests; external users complete requester details before the ticket enters distribution.

monitor.tabs: Switch between the views available to your role: global operations, distribution queues, sector workload, and your personal/requester workload. Each tab is server-filtered for your permissions.
monitor.refresh: Refresh is placed with the report controls so it updates the operational data without competing with the page title or tutorial button.
monitor.kpis: These KPI tiles summarize the selected scope: active work, completed work, stale tickets, reopened items, and other counts the current role is allowed to inspect.
monitor.workload: Workload details compare active and completed tickets per assignee. Chiefs and admins use this to spot overload, unassigned work, and distribution imbalance.
monitor.range: Change the time window for the trend chart. Short windows are useful during incidents; longer windows show whether throughput is improving.
monitor.cache: The created-vs-closed chart shows ticket intake and closure trends for the selected window. Monitor aggregates are cached briefly, so repeated reads stay fast.

admin.kpis: These cards summarize system administration: total users, active sessions from the 5-minute presence window, sectors, tickets, and current operational counters.
admin.tabs: Use the tabs to manage users, sector membership, metadata configuration, widget catalogue entries, and system health. Each change is still permission-checked on the API.

create.beneficiary: The beneficiary type is derived from your account role. Internal users file requests for internal beneficiaries; external users provide requester contact details.
create.title: The title is the queue-friendly summary shown in lists, dashboards, and review screens. Keep it short enough for operators to scan.
create.description: The description is the operational detail: request, impact, affected assets, expected outcome, and anything the reviewer needs for routing.
create.actions: Cancel returns to the queue without saving. Create submits the ticket, then opens the new ticket detail page after the backend accepts it.

audit.filters: Use quick search for a ticket UUID or actor username, then add a date range when investigating a specific incident window.
audit.table: The audit table lists immutable events. Expand a row to inspect before/after values, request metadata, correlation id, and related ticket links.
audit.refresh: Refresh reloads the audit ledger with the current filters, useful while following live activity during triage.

dashboard.list: This is your personal dashboard library. Each dashboard can hold a different operational view, such as morning review, sector workload, or admin health.
dashboard.new: Create a blank dashboard, then add widgets manually or use auto-configuration inside the dashboard.
dashboard.grid: Widgets live on a draggable, resizable grid. Each widget keeps its own title, optional legend, size, and configuration.
dashboard.add: Add Widget opens the catalogue. Auto-configuration chooses useful widgets based on your roles and sector memberships.
dashboard.arrange: Auto-arrange lays out the current widgets into a clean grid without changing their data configuration.

profile.identity: Your identity card shows the profile values synchronized from the identity provider and used in tickets, audit rows, and notifications.
profile.roles: Roles and sector memberships explain what you can see and do. Chief memberships unlock sector workload tools; admin and auditor roles unlock broader views.
profile.assignments: Recent assignments show the latest tickets routed to you, with relative update times for quick follow-up.
profile.status: The status chart summarizes your visible ticket workload by workflow state, so you can see where your work is concentrated.

tour.language: Switch between English and Romanian here. Your choice is remembered.

errors.rate_limited: Too many requests, please slow down.
errors.permission_denied: You don't have permission to do that.
